commit.message:
feat: three-tier scraping, Ahrefs API fix, and report improvements

- Add curl_cffi (Chrome TLS impersonation) as primary scraper for anti-bot bypass
- Add bot-block detection (_is_blocked) to prevent Access Denied pages in reports
- Keep requests as Tier 2 fallback, Playwright+stealth as Tier 3
- Fix Ahrefs top-pages API: use valid columns (raw_url, top_keyword_volume)
- Enhance Gemini prompt with stronger analytical instructions
- Update client profile auto-generation logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix supervisor not picking up new workflow files after pull (#27)

Re-discover workflow files from workflows/ directory after each git pull
instead of using the list captured at startup. This ensures new .md files
added via PRs are included on restart.

When explicit workflow files are passed as CLI args, the behavior is
unchanged (uses the provided list).

Add blog post: The App He Built on His Walk Home

New post exploring contract-first development (ACDCs) through the lens of
a teenager who assembles a friend challenge app on-the-fly by composing
existing KERI credentials — no code, no servers, no migration.

Includes hero image: work-worn hands stacking sealed envelopes, each with
a distinct deep indigo wax seal, on a worn oak kitchen table.

commit.message:
feat(integrations): add Google ADK adapter and converter (#184)

* feat(adapters): add Google ADK adapter and converter

Add GoogleADKAdapter using SimpleAdapter pattern with Google's Agent
Development Kit for Gemini-powered agents. Includes converter, tool
bridging via BaseTool subclass, conformance test registration, unit
tests (47 new), examples, and optional dependency configuration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review feedback for Google ADK adapter

- Use try/finally for runner.close() to prevent resource leak on error
- Remove unused google_api_key parameter
- Remove unused self._runner instance attribute
- Defensively cast output to str in _format_history_transcript
- Add from __future__ import annotations to setup_logging.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add name/description annotations for pyrefly visibility

Pyrefly cannot see inherited attributes from dynamically-imported
BaseTool. Declare name and description as class-level annotations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address code review findings for Google ADK adapter

- Narrow bare except to AttributeError in _report_event
- Add explicit thought/error message type skipping in converter
- Document mutable reference pattern in adapter docstring
- Clarify per-message runner creation rationale
- Fix transcript formatting (missing space before parens)
- Normalize test mock patterns for consistency
- Add tests for thought and error message skipping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: rename _import_adk to _require_adk and clarify eager import intent

The function was misleadingly named "lazy import" but is called at module
level by necessity (BaseTool needed at class-definition time). Renamed to
_require_adk with an accurate docstring explaining why and how the lazy
__getattr__ in adapters/__init__.py still protects unrelated imports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: eliminate shared mutable refs and improve test coverage in ADK adapter

- Replace _tools_ref/_custom_tools_ref shared mutable lists with direct
  references passed to _ThenvoiToolBridge, removing concurrency hazard
- Extract _convert_parameters to module-level _strip_additional_properties
- Type _require_adk return as tuple[type, type, type, Any]
- Fix wrong # noqa: E501 → # noqa: B901 in test async generator
- Add tests for _report_event, contacts_msg injection (36 tests, was 32)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address code review findings for Google ADK adapter

- Extract _MAX_TOOL_OUTPUT_PREVIEW constant for transcript truncation
- Handle top-level list input in _strip_additional_properties
- Add google_adk_agent entry to agent_config.yaml.example
- Tighten google-adk version constraint to >=1.0.0,<2
- Add clarifying comments for session/runner lifecycle and concurrency
- Add tests for top-level list and non-dict _strip_additional_properties

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add missing converter export, fix list recursion, and document API key

- Register GoogleADKHistoryConverter and GoogleADKMessages in
  converters/__init__.py (TYPE_CHECKING, __all__, __getattr__)
- Fix inconsistent list recursion in _strip_additional_properties
  to match the top-level handler
- Add GOOGLE_API_KEY requirement to example docstrings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: simplify schema stripping and add missing converter docstring entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove dead session reuse, clarify prompt naming, and scope custom tools

- Always generate fresh session IDs (InMemoryRunner is ephemeral per message)
- Rename system_prompt to _system_prompt_override to avoid confusion with _system_prompt
- Pass empty custom_tools to platform tool bridges (only custom bridges need the list)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add ADK version note, simplify converter branch, and test _get_declaration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add history accumulation, orphan patching, and ValidationError handling

- Maintain per-room message history for transcript injection across all
  messages (not just bootstrap), fixing conversation context loss
- Capture ADK final text response and append to room history
- Catch pydantic.ValidationError separately in tool bridge with formatted
  field-level error messages
- Add _patch_orphaned_tool_calls in converter for interrupted tool execution
- Safer dict(fc.args) handling in _report_event
- Add 26 new tests covering history accumulation, final response capture,
  sync/multiple custom tools, ValidationError, orphan patching, and edge cases
- Fix conformance config comment and add more expected_initial_values
- Add missing from __future__ import annotations in setup_logging.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add ValueError handler and resilient event reporting in ADK adapter

Catch ValueError separately from generic Exception in tool bridge to
properly handle custom tool validation errors. Wrap event reporting in
try-except so reporting failures don't crash message processing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add sliding window to ADK adapter history to prevent unbounded growth

Add max_history_messages parameter (default 50) to GoogleADKAdapter,
matching the CodexAdapter pattern. Only the most recent N messages are
injected as transcript context, preventing unbounded token cost growth
in long-running conversations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add Google ADK converter integration tests

Add 6 integration tests validating GoogleADKHistoryConverter against
real platform data: tool history round-trip, parallel tool call
batching, thought/error event skipping, is_error flag preservation,
and multi-turn conversation flow with role alternation verification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip inserted synthetic message in orphan patching loop

The `_patch_orphaned_tool_calls` while-loop inserted a synthetic
message at `i + 1` but did not explicitly advance past it, relying
on the implicit `i += 1` at the end of the loop body.  Add an
explicit skip so the intent is clear and future modifications
cannot accidentally re-examine the synthetic message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: trim unbounded room history, pass custom_tools consistently, add skip guard

- Trim _room_history when it exceeds 2x max_history_messages to prevent
  unbounded memory growth in long-lived rooms
- Pass self._custom_tools to all tool bridges instead of empty list for
  platform tool bridges
- Add pytest.importorskip guard so adapter tests skip gracefully when
  google-adk is not installed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: reduce session log noise and add concurrency test for ADK adapter

- Downgrade per-message session creation log from INFO to DEBUG
- Add concurrent on_message test for different rooms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: defer google-adk imports to adapter instantiation time

Move _require_adk() from module-level execution to a @lru_cache lazy
call, and wrap _ThenvoiToolBridge in a cached factory function. This
ensures importing the module no longer triggers google-adk imports —
the dependency is only resolved when GoogleADKAdapter is instantiated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add character-based transcript truncation to prevent token limit overflow

Adds max_transcript_chars (default 100k) to GoogleADKAdapter to cap the
injected history transcript size. When exceeded, the transcript is trimmed
from the beginning to the character limit and snapped to the next newline
boundary, with a warning logged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: guard against missing id key in orphan patching and use uv add in error message

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: harden ADK adapter against internal API changes and document concurrency guarantees

- Wrap _get_declaration in try/except with clear RuntimeError for google-adk internal API breakage
- Document per-room serialization guarantee from ExecutionContext (no lock needed)
- Document that converter structured output is consumed for transcript formatting, not passed to ADK directly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: improve ADK adapter robustness and conformance coverage

- Carry tool name in synthetic orphan function_response blocks
- Add proactive hasattr check for BaseTool._get_declaration internal API
- Use getattr guards consistently for ADK event attribute access
- Enable on_started conformance test (no live client needed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace fragile _get_declaration override with candidate-based probing

Replace the hardcoded `hasattr(BaseTool, '_get_declaration')` check with
a candidate-based probing mechanism that detects and overrides all known
declaration method names. This makes the tool bridge resilient to ADK
renaming the internal API in future releases.

Key changes:
- Add _DECLARATION_CANDIDATES tuple with known method names
- Eagerly build and cache FunctionDeclaration in __init__
- Override all detected methods via setattr at class creation
- Add smoke-test to verify declaration mechanism end-to-end

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: pre-create ADK session before run_async to avoid missing session errors

The ADK runner's run_async expects the session to already exist in the
InMemorySessionService. Pre-creating it avoids a lookup failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback for Google ADK adapter

- Move try/finally to wrap from runner creation to prevent resource leak
  if create_session or content assembly raises before run_async
- Use logger.exception() in catch-all tool handler for stack traces
- Include tool name in generic error message returned to LLM
- Use .pop() for _room_sessions cleanup (consistent with _room_history)
- Add logger.debug for skipped events in execution reporting
- Use logger.exception() in on_message error handler (style consistency)
- Add cleanup edge-case tests (idempotent double-cleanup, multi-room isolation)
- Add third example (03_custom_tools.py) for INT-210 acceptance criteria
- Update README.md with Google ADK section, install command, and examples table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat: add two-layer evaluation model (screen check + fit score)

Layer 1 (Screen Check) extracts hard gate requirements from the JD
and checks each as Pass/Partial/Fail before any dimensional scoring.
Produces a screen verdict: Likely Passes Screen, Borderline, or
Likely Filtered Out. This prevents false hope by surfacing screening
barriers upfront.

Layer 2 (Fit Score) runs dimensional scoring as before, but output
framing changes based on screen verdict. Score labeled "if screened
in" when candidate would likely be filtered out.

No consumer job-matching tool separates these layers. Most produce
single composite scores where strengths mask critical gaps.

Changes:
- SKILL.md: 5-stage pipeline, Section 0 output, screen-aware framing
- scoring-heuristics.md: full screen check methodology and rules
- ARCHITECTURE.md: updated all 3 Mermaid diagrams for new flow
- README.md: 10 sections, 5 stages, new tradeoff section
- sample-output.md: added screen check showing Borderline verdict

commit.message:
fix(bingo): add gameState to animation useEffect deps to prevent stale closure

The animation effect checked gameState === DRAWING but gameState was not
in the dependency array, causing a stale closure where the effect fired
with the pre-update WAITING_VRF value and returned early — ball draw
animation never started.

commit.message:
fix(ci): use env(VERSION) instead of strenv("VERSION") in yq

strenv("VERSION") looks for a var literally named "VERSION" (with quotes),
resulting in empty values. env(VERSION) reads the env var correctly.

commit.message:
fix: ROM downloader hangup prevention + Batocera v38 support

- Lower version gate v43 → v38 (Python 3.9+, core pinning stable on v38)
- wget --timeout=30 prevents indefinite stall on slow archive.org reads
- MAX_DL_SECONDS=1200 hard-kills any download exceeding 20 minutes
- Real-time progress: stat .dl_tmp file every 5s, report actual percent
- "verifying" status emitted between os.rename and dat_checker so UI
  doesn't appear frozen at 100% during hash verification
- Stale DOWNLOAD_FLAG cleanup: worker clears flag if >25 min old
  (handles crashed subprocess that left flag behind)
- fix) dispatch now checks DOWNLOAD_FLAG before spawning
  process_download_queue — prevents boot-scan/repair race

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add smart list continuation on Enter in editor (#21)

When pressing Enter on a line with a list prefix (bullets, ordered lists,
checkboxes), automatically continue the list on the next line with matching
indentation and prefix. Ordered list numbers are auto-incremented and
subsequent items are renumbered. Pressing Enter on an empty list item
(just the prefix with no content) removes the prefix, ending the list.

Supported prefixes: "- ", "* ", "1. ", "- [ ] ", "- [x] "

commit.message:
fix: increase extension max file count to 150 (#671)

## Summary

- The extension safety analyzer's `MAX_FILE_COUNT` was set to 100, which
blocked pushing larger service extensions like `@swamp/aws/ec2` (104
models)
- Increased the limit from 100 to 150 to accommodate extensions that
cover broad service APIs
- Updated the corresponding test, design doc, and skill reference to
match

## Test Plan

- [x] Updated test now creates 151 files and asserts the limit is 150
- [x] All 111 extension domain tests pass
- [x] `deno fmt --check` passes
- [x] `deno lint` passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
16 scraper improvements: search terms, anti-bot fidelity, data quality, resilience

Bottle matching: Stagg Jr expanded terms (stagg bourbon/kentucky), Thomas Handy
short form, Old Forester President short form. isFirstQuery race condition fixed
by using .map() index instead of mutable flag across concurrent tasks.

Anti-bot: Chrome 136-accurate Accept header (avif/webp/signed-exchange),
Sec-Fetch-User: ?1 header added.

Data quality: Safeway price .toFixed(2), parseSize centiliter support (75cl→750ml),
formatStoreInfo strips duplicate retailer prefix, dead code removed from Costco fetch.

Resilience: zipToCoords AbortSignal.timeout(10s), Kroger promise .catch() in
discoverStores prevents unhandled rejection, fetchRetry console.warn on first
failure, Total Wine brace-counting INITIAL_STATE parser (handles </script> in
JSON strings).

Discord: truncateTitle (256 chars) on all embed titles, truncateDescription on
summary embed. 369 tests, 91.8% branch coverage.

commit.message:
fix(sim): PR #580 deferred items — rope_scaling extraction, MaxModelLen int64, tests, docs (#606)

Complete 7 deferred hardening items from issue #580 (PR #587 handoff):

1. Extract applyRopeScaling as a pure function with 26 table-driven test
   cases covering blacklist (su/longrope/llama3), mrope fall-through,
   gemma3 substring match (handles text_config pivot), yarn original base,
   overflow guards, NaN/Inf defense, degenerate inputs.

2. Change MaxModelLen from int to int64 for consistency with ProgressIndex,
   TotalKVBlocks, BlockSizeTokens. Updates 6 type sites, removes redundant
   int64() casts, adds int64() widening at EnqueueRequest comparison sites.

3. Add cluster-mode MaxModelLen drop test (BC-6): Guard 1a (input >= limit)
   and Guard 1b (input + budget > limit), INV-1 conservation, inFlightRequests
   drain, Metrics.Requests map cleanup.

4. Add chunked prefill + MaxModelLen interaction test (BC-7): verifies no
   spurious force-completion during multi-chunk prefill (TotalOutputTokens=49,
   LengthCappedRequests=0, TTFT recorded).

5. Add glossary entries for MaxModelLen and Oracle Knowledge Boundary (INV-9).

6. Refine rope_scaling documentation with explicit blacklist details.

7. Fix pre-existing gemma3 bug: ParseHFConfig's text_config pivot overwrites
   model_type from "gemma3" to "gemma3_text", making the exact-match check
   dead code. Changed to strings.Contains to match vLLM's substring semantics.

Related to #580. Discovered issues: #602, #603, #604, #605.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix linarith failure in equilibriumMaxwellian_T_unique

linarith cannot derive T₁ = T₂ from 2*π*T₁ = 2*π*T₂ because π is not
a literal — it needs nonlinear reasoning. Switch to nlinarith with
Real.pi_pos hint.

perf(m1): PGO-optimized build pipeline for assignment service (#116)

Add 3-phase Profile-Guided Optimization build:
- Phase 1: Instrument binary with -Cprofile-generate
- Phase 2: Run realistic gRPC workload (11.5K calls across all
  experiment types: AB, SESSION_LEVEL, MAB, CONTEXTUAL_BANDIT,
  Team Draft, Optimized, Multileave interleaving)
- Phase 3: Optimize with -Cprofile-use for LLVM-guided hot path tuning

Changes:
- scripts/pgo_build.sh: 3-phase PGO orchestrator with graceful
  fallback to standard --release if llvm-profdata unavailable
- scripts/pgo_workload.sh: realistic workload hitting all 9 dev
  experiments (replaces stub)
- scripts/validate_assignment_p99.sh: SLA validation against criterion
  benchmarks (p99 < 5ms assignment, < 15ms interleaving)
- Cargo.toml: panic = "abort" in release profile (crash-only design,
  eliminates unwinder overhead)
- rust-toolchain.toml: add llvm-tools component for llvm-profdata
- Justfile: add pgo-build and build-assignment-release recipes
- nightly.yml: add experimentation-assignment benchmarks to CI

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Sprites adapter for Fly.io Firecracker microVMs (#2)

Add sprites() adapter wrapping @fly/sprites SDK, using
sprite.execFile('sh', ['-c', cmd]) for proper shell parsing (the SDK's
exec() does naive whitespace splitting). File ops use base64 pipe via sh.

Also extends ServerConfigOpts with 18 new sections (gRPC, audit, DLP,
proxy, cgroups, etc.) and adds spritesDefaults() returning
Sprites-optimized SecureConfig. Includes standalone e2e test runner
(vitest incompatible with sprites native WebSocket).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Migrate year-length formulas to Fourier harmonics with derived means

Replace linear obliquity/eccentricity year-length formulas with Fourier
harmonic decomposition fitted from 491 data points (±25,000 years).
Means are now derived from inputmeanlengthsolaryearindays via H ratios,
so only harmonic coefficients need refitting if H changes.

Updated across all codebases: script.js, constants.js, orbital-engine.js,
predictive_formula.py, constants_scripts.py, and documentation.
Rewrote docs/11 from 2584-line work journal to ~295-line reference doc.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(m3): end-to-end latency SLA validation tests (#118)

* feat(m3): add Kafka-driven surrogate recalibration consumer

Bridge M5's RecalibrationRequest Kafka messages to M3's RecalibrationJob.
Consumer reads from surrogate_recalibration_requests topic, resolves
model_id → experiment_id(s) via ConfigStore, and triggers recalibration
with at-least-once delivery semantics.

- New recalconsumer package with processMessage() extracted for testability
- GetExperimentsByModelID reverse lookup on ConfigStore
- 7 new tests (5 consumer + 2 config)
- Wired into main.go startup lifecycle

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(m3): add end-to-end latency SLA validation tests

7 tests validating daily (2h) and guardrail (30min) SLA compliance
using query count as primary proxy (90s/query production average):
- Daily pipeline: 42 queries total (< 80 budget)
- Guardrail pipeline: 2 queries total (< 20 budget)
- Per-experiment breakdown with exact job-type counts
- Wall-clock timing with 1ms/query mock (catches O(n^2))
- Per-metric-type query formula verification
- Linear scaling validation with 20-experiment synthetic config

This completes the last Phase 4 onboarding checklist item for M3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: visual grouping — raise cluster threshold and add section dividers

Two issues fixed in the EditionPicker visual mode:

1. Obvious matches getting split: clusterByHash threshold raised from 10
   to 15 Hamming bits, which correctly merges same-cover variants that
   differ only in resolution or minor compression artifacts.

2. No visual separation between clusters: replaced the flat grid with
   sectioned groups (same layout as publisher mode). Each distinct cover
   design gets a "Cover design N" heading and its own sub-grid, so
   visually different covers are clearly separated.

Also fixed the cluster ordering logic to use the minimum cover_id in
the cluster (not just the first-seen) for stable, consistent ordering.
